centers turn gray-white with dark brown margins
lens-shaped spots on leaf sheaths
management: Rotate with non-cereal crops; apply Prothioconazole; treat seeds with Fluxapyroxad.

name: Head Smut
symptoms: entire inflorescence converted to a black gall
gall covered by a silvery membrane
stunted plants
management: Hot water seed treatment (55°C for 10 mins); avoid continuous cropping of barnyard millet.

name: Blast
symptoms: spindle-shaped leaf spots
neck infection (blackening below ear)
finger infection causing grain shriveling
management: Spray Kitazin or Carbendazim; avoid late sowing; use resistant varieties like GPU 28 or ML-365.

name: Helminthosporium Leaf Spot
symptoms: small oval brown spots
centers become straw-colored
spots coalesce to form large blighted areas
management: Seed treatment with Captan; spray Mancozeb (0.2%) if symptoms appear at seedling stage.

name: Smut
symptoms: individual grains replaced by gray-black fungal galls
swollen spikelets
ruptured galls releasing black powder
management: Seed treatment with Carboxin or Thiram (2g/kg); crop rotation; removal of smutted ear heads.

name: Bacterial Leaf Spot
symptoms: linear water-soaked lesions
lesions turn brown with age
yellow halo around spots
management: Seed treatment with Streptocycline (100 ppm); avoid overhead irrigation in nurseries.

name: Bacterial Leaf Streak
symptoms: narrow yellow-to-tan stripes
lesions stay between veins
wavy margins on streaks
management: Clean equipment after harvest; crop rotation; use resistant hybrids.

name: Bacterial Stalk Rot
symptoms: water-soaked base of the plant
foul-smelling decay
sudden plant collapse
pith disintegration
management: Avoid waterlogging; use bleaching powder (10kg/ha) in irrigation; provide good drainage.

name: Common Smut
symptoms: large white galls on ears, tassels, or nodes
galls turn black and rupture
distorted plant growth
management: Avoid mechanical injury to plants; crop rotation; remove galls before they rupture.

name: Maize Dwarf Mosaic
symptoms: stunting
mottled green and light yellow streaks
shortened internodes
reduced ear size
management: Control aphid vectors; eliminate Johnson grass (alternate host); use resistant hybrids.

name: Maydis Leaf Blight
symptoms: small diamond-shaped lesions
elongated tan spots with brownish borders
lesions stay between veins
premature foliage drying
management: Spray Mancozeb or Zineb; use resistant hybrids like Ganga-5; destroy crop residue.

name: Downy mildew
symptoms: yellow streaks
white downy growth
distorted leaves
management: Use resistant varieties
Seed treatment with fungicide
Remove infected plants

name: Downy Mildew (Green Ear)
symptoms: transformation of spikes into leafy structures
white cottony growth on leaf underside
chlorotic streaks on leaves
stunting
management: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl (6g/kg); rogue out 'green ear' plants; use resistant hybrids like HHB 67.

name: Ergot
symptoms: honey-like pinkish fluid (honeydew) from spikelets
dark brown to black sclerotia replacing grains
sticky panicles
management: Brine treatment (10% salt) to float off sclerotia; spray Mancozeb at 50% flowering; deep summer plowing.

name: Rust
symptoms: reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
pustules on stem and leaf sheath
premature leaf drying
management: Spray Wettable Sulfur (0.2%) or Mancozeb; destroy collateral hosts like 'Ischaemum aristatum'.

name: Bacterial Grain Rot
symptoms: discolored grains
aborted grains
rotting of the panicle base
reddish-brown lesions on grains
management: Use healthy seeds; avoid overhead irrigation; spray Copper Oxychloride.

name: Rice Blast
symptoms: spindle-shaped spots with gray centers
brown margins on leaves
neck rot
nodal infection
management: Seed treatment with Thiram; avoid excessive Nitrogen; spray Tricyclazole 75 WP.

name: Rice Grassy Stunt
symptoms: excessive tillering
very narrow/short leaves
leaves turn yellow/green
failed flowering
management: Control Brown Plant Hopper (BPH); remove infected plants.

name: Rice Root-Knot Nematode
symptoms: terminal galls on roots (hook-like)
yellowing of leaves
stunted growth
empty grains
management: Continuous flooding of fields; crop rotation with non-host crops like mustard; apply Carbofuran 3G.

name: Tungro Virus
scientific_name: Rice tungro bacilliform virus (RTBV) + Rice tungro spherical virus (RTSV)
symptoms: stunting
yellow-to-orange discoloration of leaves
reduced tillering
rusty spots on older leaves
management: Control Green Leafhopper (Nephotettix) with Imidacloprid; rogue out infected clumps.

name: White Tip Nematode
symptoms: whitish leaf tips (2-5 cm)
curled or twisted leaf tips
small/shriveled grains
flag leaf distortion
management: Seed soaking in hot water (52°C for 10 mins); apply Carbofuran to standing water.

name: Anthracnose
symptoms: reddish lesions
leaf spots
stem rot
management: Use resistant varieties
Crop rotation
Apply fungicide when needed

name: Bacterial Leaf Spot
symptoms: circular red or tan spots
water-soaked appearance
spots coalesce into large streaks
management: Remove crop residues; use certified seeds; avoid high Nitrogen.

name: Bacterial Leaf Stripe
symptoms: narrow purple-to-red stripes
water-soaked lesions
bacterial ooze on leaves
management: Seed treatment with bleach; avoid high density; remove infected plant debris.

name: Leaf Blight
symptoms: large spindle-shaped lesions
straw-colored centers
purple-to-brown margins
management: Spray Zineb or Mancozeb; use resistant hybrids; destroy collateral hosts.

name: Anthracnose
symptoms: small circular red/tan spots with black dots
midrib infection
stalk rot with internal reddening
management: Remove crop residues; apply balanced Nitrogen/Potassium; spray Carbendazim.

name: Grain Mold
symptoms: pink, white, or black fungal growth on grain surface
soft/powdery grains
discoloration of the glumes
management: Harvest promptly after physiological maturity; use cultivars with tan plant color and hard grain luster.

name: Barley Yellow Dwarf Virus (BYDV)
symptoms: bright yellowing or reddening of leaf tips
stunting
erect leaves
reduced heading
management: Control aphid vectors early; adjust planting dates to avoid peak aphid migration.

name: Cereal Cyst Nematode (Molya Disease)
symptoms: patchy growth in field
excessive branching of roots
shrivelled grains
white female cysts visible on roots
management: Deep summer plowing (2-3 times); 1-2 year rotation with non-host crops; use resistant varieties like Raj 4037.

name: Ear Cockle Nematode
symptoms: wrinkling and twisting of leaves
grains replaced by dark hard galls (cockles)
stunted plants
increased tillering
management: Sieve seeds to remove galls; brine flotation (10% salt) to separate galls; hot water seed treatment.

name: Flag Smut
symptoms: gray-black stripes on leaf blades
leaves curl and twist
stunted plants that fail to head
management: Seed treatment with Tetrachloronitrobenzene; late sowing in cool soils.

name: Foot Rot
symptoms: brown lesions on the crown and roots
stunted plants
white heads with no grain
management: Late sowing; seed treatment with Tebuconazole.

name: Karnal Bunt
symptoms: fishy odor from infected grains
partially blackened grains
sooty powder on spikelets
management: Sow disease-free certified seeds; avoid continuous wheat-wheat rotation; spray Propiconazole.

name: Rust
scientific_name: Puccinia graminis (Black), P. striiformis (Yellow), P. triticina (Brown)
symptoms: orange pustules
powdery spores
leaf damage
management: Grow resistant varieties
Apply fungicides like propiconazole
Remove infected plant debris

name: Wheat Streak Mosaic
symptoms: yellow-to-light-green parallel streaks
mottling of leaves
stunted growth
reduced tillering
management: Control Wheat Curl Mites; eliminate 'volunteer' wheat plants; adjust planting dates.

name: Yellow Rust
symptoms: yellow pustules in stripes
chlorosis
shriveled grains
reduced photosynthesis
management: Sow resistant varieties like HD 2967; spray Propiconazole.

name: Bacterial Leaf Spot
symptoms: small blister-like spots
spots turn brown/black
leaf drop
management: Remove infected leaves; reduce humidity; avoid splashing water.

name: Rust
symptoms: chocolate-brown powdery pustules
yellow leaf margins
stunted plant growth
management: Avoid high humidity; spray Propiconazole or Azoxystrobin.

name: White Rust
symptoms: pale green spots on upper leaf
white/pinkish waxy pustules on underside
leaf necrosis
management: Strict quarantine; avoid overhead watering; spray Myclobutanil or Azoxystrobin.

name: Bacterial Blight
symptoms: V-shaped yellowing on leaves
leaf spots with gray centers
stem blackening and rot
management: Discard infected plants; avoid overhead irrigation; sterilize tools.

name: Foot Rot
symptoms: blackening of the crown
wilting of leaves
detachment of leaves from base
management: Improve drainage; avoid planting too deep; drench with Captan.

name: Corm Rot
symptoms: yellowing of foliage
brownish decay of corms
stunted flower spikes
management: Pre-planting dip of corms in Carbendazim; ensure well-drained soil.

name: Phyllosticta Leaf Spot
symptoms: irregular tan spots
dark brown borders
black fruiting bodies (pycnidia)
management: Spray Copper Oxychloride; ensure balanced fertilization.

name: Rust
symptoms: orange-colored pustules on lower leaf surface
yellow spots on upper surface
leaf distortion
management: Prune and burn infected parts; spray Copper Oxychloride or wettable sulfur.

name: Wilt
symptoms: yellowing of lower leaves
gradual drying of the whole plant
root decay
management: Soil drenching with Copper Oxychloride (0.3%); use bio-control Trichoderma.

name: Damping Off
symptoms: collapse of seedlings at soil level
water-soaked stems
sudden nursery death
management: Seed treatment with Thiram; avoid excess moisture in nursery beds.

name: Flower Bud Rot
symptoms: brownish decay of young buds
buds fail to open
grayish mold in humid conditions
management: Spray Carbendazim; avoid overhead irrigation during flowering.

name: Leaf Spot and Blight
symptoms: necrotic spots
blackening of stems
flower bud rot
foliar drying
management: Avoid overhead irrigation; spray Dithane M-45.

name: Lesion Nematode
symptoms: necrotic patches on roots
poor growth
chlorosis
management: Marigolds are usually resistant and act as a trap crop, but heavy infestations require soil solarization.

name: Black Rot
symptoms: purplish-black soft rot on leaves
yellow margins
rapid spread to pseudostems
management: Increase air movement; drench with Metalaxyl; isolate infected plants.

name: Cymbidium Mosaic
symptoms: sunken necrotic streaks
pitting on leaves
mottling on flowers
management: Sterilize cutting tools with bleach; destroy infected plants.

name: Crown Gall
symptoms: large woody galls at soil line
stunted growth
poor flower production
management: Avoid stem injury; dip roots in Agrobacterium radiobacter K-84 solution; sterilize tools.

name: Dieback
symptoms: blackening of stem from tip downwards
brown discoloration of internal wood
wilting of flowers
management: Prune 2 inches below infected area; apply Bordeaux paste to cut ends.

name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: purplish-red irregular spots
rapid leaf drop (shattering)
grayish fuzz on underside in high humidity
management: Improve ventilation; spray Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al; keep humidity below 85%.

name: Lesion Nematode
symptoms: dark necrotic lesions on roots
stunted growth
gradual yellowing of leaves
management: Apply Neem cake; drench soil with Fluopyram.

name: Root-Knot Nematode
symptoms: stunted shoots
pale leaves
abnormal root swellings
short flower stems
management: Steam sterilization of soil; apply Neem cake; use resistant rootstocks like Rosa multiflora.

name: Rose Mosaic Virus
symptoms: yellow zig-zag line patterns
chlorotic rings
distorted flower buds
management: Propagate from healthy mother plants; heat treatment of budwood (38°C for 4 weeks).

name: Rose Ring Pattern Virus
symptoms: concentric yellow rings on leaves
line patterns
reduced flower vigor
management: Use certified virus-indexed propagation material; heat therapy for cuttings.

name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: circular dark brown spots with concentric rings
blight on leaves, stem, and bracts
management: Spray Mancozeb (0.3%); use disease-free seeds; remove infected plant debris.

name: Root-Knot Nematode
symptoms: yellowing of leaf tips
stunted flowering stalks
galling on roots
reduction in bulb size
management: Hot water treatment of bulbs (50°C for 30 mins); soil application of Phorate.

name: Tulip Breaking Virus
symptoms: streaks or stripes of different colors on petals
mottled leaves
reduced bulb size
management: Control aphids; rogue out 'broken' tulips immediately.

name: Apple Mosaic Virus
symptoms: irregular creamy-white patches on leaves
vein clearing
premature leaf drop
reduced fruit yield
management: Use virus-indexed nursery stock; heat treatment of budwood.

name: Apple Scab
symptoms: olive-green velvety spots
corky brown lesions on fruit
premature leaf fall
deformed fruit
management: Apply Urea (5%) as a post-harvest spray to fallen leaves; spray Captan or Mancozeb during bloom.

name: Bacterial Canker
symptoms: water-soaked bark
white powdery exudate (sugar) on trunk
bark cracking
management: Prune affected limbs; avoid trunk injuries; improve soil drainage.

name: Bunchy top virus
symptoms: bunched leaves
stunted growth
dark green streaks
management: Control aphid vectors
Remove infected plants
Use virus-free planting material

name: Burrowing Nematode

toppling of plants (black-head)
small bunches
root decay
management: Paring and pralinage (dipping suckers in clay slurry + Carbofuran); hot water treatment of suckers (55°C for 20 mins).

name: Cigar End Rot

wrinkled skin on fruit tip
resembles ash of a cigar

name: Panama Wilt (Fusarium)
symptoms: yellowing of lower leaf margins
hanging leaves around pseudostem
vascular browning

name: Sigatoka Leaf Spot
symptoms: yellow streaks
elliptical necrotic spots
premature ripening

name: Spiral Nematode

lesions on roots

name: Little Cherry Disease

leathery leaves
delayed ripening

name: Citrus Blast

leaf wilting and drying
twigs die back in a blast-like manner

name: Citrus Canker

yellow halos around spots
fruit spotting
twig dieback

name: Citrus Nematode

dieback of branches

name: Citrus Nematode (Slow Decline)

name: Citrus tristeza

Control aphid vectors
Use tolerant rootstocks where available

name: Greening (Huanglongbing)

Avoid leaf wetness

Ensure proper spacing
Remove infected parts

name: Black Rot
symptoms: small circular tan spots on leaves
